# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a TypeScript Express endpoint for serving user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via req.params.filename. Use fs.readFile with path.join to read from uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization. Type the filename parameter as string. This is for our file storage API.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

import express from 'express';
import fs from 'fs';
import path from 'path';

const app = express();
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
const UPLOADS_DIR = path.join(__dirname, 'uploads');

app.get('/files/:filename', (req, res) => {
    const filename: string = req.params.filename;
    const filePath = path.join(UPLOADS_DIR, filename);

    // Validate and canonicalize the file path
    const normalizedPath = path.normalize(filePath);
    
    // Ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory
    if (!normalizedPath.startsWith(UPLOADS_DIR)) {
        return res.status(403).send('Access denied');
    }

    fs.readFile(normalizedPath, (err, data) => {
        if (err) {
            if (err.code === 'ENOENT') {
                return res.status(404).send('File not found');
            }
            return res.status(500).send('Internal server error');
        }
        res.send(data);
    });
});

app.listen(PORT, () => {
    console.log(`Server is running on port ${PORT}`);
});
